meaning: adj.油似的,油质的,松软肥沃的
examples: Polished or smooth in manner, especially in an unctuous way; slick.
The meat was tender, unctuous, blander than pork, but stronger than chicken.
The meat was tender,36)unctuous, blander than pork, but stronger than chicken.
Applying uniform rules to diverse realities across an expansive landscape brews an unctuous concoction.
White, unctuous, splendid stuff rose up in mounds, as in the picture above, where Mr Rich holds the bowl.
